folio.label: 174v
folio.l: of wrathe , he seide in this wyse

¢ Dame I preye I I ©ow , æ
©e be nat displesed , of thynges æ I seye ,

for I knowe wel ,
æ I am angry & wrootú , & æ is no wonder ,

& they æ ben
wrothe , witen nat wel , what they doon , ne what æei seyn

¢ Therfore the ¸phete seitú ; troubled eyen , han noo cleer
sigúte ;

But seitú & cou~seiletú me as ©ow liketú , for I am
redy to doo , rigút as ©e wol desire ,

And if ©e repreeue me
of my folie , I am the more holden , to loue ©ow & to preise
©ow

¢ For Salamon seitú ; æ he that repäuetú hi~ , æ dotú
folie ,

he shal fynde grettere grace , than he , æ deceyuetú hi~
by swete wordes

¢ Tha~ne seide Dame Pèdence , I make
no semblau~t of wrathe ne of angir , but for ©oure grete ¸fit

¢ For Salamon seitú ; he is more wortú , æ repäuetú or
chidetú a fool for his folie , shewynge hi~ semblau~t of wra-
the ,

than he æ supportetú hi~ & preisetú hi~ , in his mysdoy~ge
& laugúetú at his folie

the fool
correctetú & amendetú hi~ self

¢ Tha~ne seide Melibee , I
shal nat konne answere ©ow , vn to so manye faire resons ,
as ©e putten to me & shewen ,

Seitú shortly ©oure wil and
©oure counseil , And I am al redy , to fulfille & ¹forme it ,,

Tha~-
ne Dame Pèdence , discouäed al hire wil to hi~ , & seide ,

I coun-
seille ©ow q¶ she a bouen alle thynges , æ ©e make pees , bitwe-
ne god & ©ow ,
& betú reconsiled vn to hi~ , & to his gce ,

For
as I haue seid ©ow heere biforn~ , god hatú suffred ©ow , to
haue this tébulaco~n & dissese for ©oure synnes ,

And if ©e do
as I seye ©ow , god wol sende ©oure aduäsaries vn to ©ow ,

&
make he~ falle at ©oure feet , redy to do ©oure wyl & ©oure co-
maundementz

¢ For Salamou~ seitú ; whan the condicion
of man , is plesau~t & likyngö to god ,

he chaungetú the hertes
of the ma~nes aduäsaries , & co~streynetú he~ , to biseken hi~ of
pees & of gce ,

I preye ©ow late me speke w ©oure aduäsaries
in pryue place ,

For they shuln nat knowe , æ it be of ©oure
wil , or ©oure assent ,

And tha~ne , whan I knowe here wil &
here entent , I may cou~seille ©ow , the moore seurly

¢ Dame
q¶ Melibe , dotú ©oure wil & ©oure likynge ,

for I putte me
holly , in ©oure disposicon and ordinau~ce

¢ Tha~ne Dame
Pèdence , whan she sey , the good wil of hire housbonde , she deli-
uered vn to hire , & tok auys in hire self ,

thynkynge how cross in the left margin/ODR/
she mygúte brynge this nede , vn to good ende ,

And whan
she sey hire tyme , she sente for æeise aduäsaries , to come
vn to hire , in to a pryue place ,

& shewed wysely vn to he~
the grete goodes æ comen of pees ,
& the grete harmes and
